description: Boot power-on sequence for M2 Pro/Max. Key difference from A2442: Austringer VR (not Viper) and CLVR (not Monaco) must power up before SoC dynamic domains are ready. 42A SVR AON design.
WARNING: Austringer replaces Viper. CLVR replaces Monaco. MPMU/SPMU buck numbers differ from A2442. Verify all buck assignments against power aliases pages 145-146.

sequence.name: USB-C Cable Insertion -- 5V Default
sequence.action: USB-C cable inserted. Charger provides default 5V. CD3217 (UF400/UF500/UG400) internal MOSFET passes 5V.

sequence.name: Charger Input Active -- PPDCIN_AON
sequence.action: Charger input path enabled. CHGR_AUX_OK asserted. I2C level translation active (page 30).

sequence.name: PPBUS_AON Established
sequence.action: Main power bus comes up from battery (12.6V) or adapter input. ARKANOID debug connector J5150 available.

sequence.name: PP3V8_AON Powers Up -- U5700 SVR AON (42A)
sequence.action: U5700 converts PPBUS_AON to PP3V8_AON (3.8V, 42A max). 3 Iceman gate driver phases. Feeds both PMUs + WiFi + display + USB-C.

sequence.name: SPMU & MPMU Startup -- AON and S2 Rails Cascade
sequence.action: Both PMUs receive PP3V8_AON and generate output rails in hardware-defined sequence.

sequence.name: Standalone VRs Power Up -- 5V and 3V3
sequence.action: UC260 generates PP5V_S2. Load switches enabled for USB-C ports and HDMI.

sequence.name: CLVR Instances Power Up -- CPU/GPU/Fabric/ANE Domains
sequence.action: 5 CLVR instances start generating dynamic SoC domains. This replaces the Viper+Monaco architecture of A2442.

sequence.name: Austringer VR Powers Up -- PCPU SRAM Domains
sequence.action: U9300 Austringer VR controller starts multi-phase power stages (5 phases). PCPU SRAM domains receive power. NOT same as A2442 Viper.

sequence.name: MPMU AWAKE Domains Active
sequence.action: MPMU AWAKE bucks generate remaining dynamic domains.

sequence.name: M2 Pro/Max Hard Start Complete -- SoC Commands CD3217
sequence.action: SoC completed OTP and hard start. Commands CD3217 to negotiate PD.

sequence.name: Soft Start -- SSD, BIOS, Display
sequence.action: SoC reads dual SSDs (SSD0 + SSD1 via Ocarina), loads firmware, display power sequencer (UP710, page 117) enables Mini-LED XDR panel, Luxe backlight (UP800) activates.
